## About This Role

Ares Management is a leading global alternative investment manager with over $430B in AUM. Ares Digital Infrastructure (ADI) is part of Ares's Real Assets platform, targeting high-quality digital infrastructure assets to capitalize on surging global demand for data, cloud computing, and artificial intelligence. ADI recently completed its inaugural US$2.4 billion data center development fund in Japan and continues to expand its global data center portfolio through its wholly-owned operating platform, Ada Infrastructure.

The team seeks an Associate in Asia to work closely with the investment team on sourcing, due diligence, and execution of digital infrastructure investment opportunities across the region. The role sits within one of the world's premier alternative investment firms and offers significant exposure to large-scale infrastructure transactions.

Key Responsibilities:

- Conduct investment research on target companies, industries, and digital infrastructure asset classes

- Build and maintain financial models: valuation, LBO analysis, DCF, and returns modeling

- Perform industry and competitive analysis to support investment theses

- Lead and coordinate business diligence across operational, financial, and market dimensions

- Draft investment memoranda for internal Investment Committee and external stakeholders

- Prepare comprehensive due diligence packs for the investment team with relevant analytics and background

Ideal candidates will have 2-4 years of experience in investment banking, private equity, or infrastructure investing, with strong quantitative and analytical skills. Experience in data centers, telecommunications, or digital infrastructure is a plus. The position offers the opportunity to join a globally recognized alternatives platform at the intersection of infrastructure investing and the AI-driven digital economy.

Apply for this Position

## About This Role

Ares Management's Secondaries team specializes in secondary market transactions of private equity, real estate, credit, and infrastructure investments, with AUM of approximately $38 billion as of September 30, 2025. The team is looking to hire an Associate, Portfolio Analytics in New York, NY or West Hartford, CT, joining a dynamic Finance team of approximately 60 professionals across West Hartford, Boston, and New York.

The role requires investment analysis, accounting, or private equity/fund of funds experience, with a solid understanding of fund reporting, partnership accounting, and performance metrics.

Key Responsibilities:

- Work with investment professionals to assist in portfolio monitoring

- Perform detailed analysis of quarterly investment report valuations, including diversification metrics and valuation gains and losses

- Prepare quarterly investor reports and performance returns at portfolio and fund levels (TWR, IRR, Multiples)

- Analyze valuation changes and drivers across portfolio investments

- Process investment amendments and consents with investment professionals

- Provide data to Investor Relations for investor presentations and fundraising efforts

Qualifications:

- Bachelor's degree in accounting or finance

- 2-3 years of experience in investment analysis, accounting, or a related field

- Strong financial analysis, writing, and presentation skills

- Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Word

- Self-motivated with strong work ethic and attention to detail

- Minimum GPA of 3.4

Ares Management is a global alternative investment manager with a 25+ year track record. Their culture is guided by core values — Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, Trustworthy — with a purpose to be a catalyst for shared prosperity.

Apply for this Position

## About This Role

Ares Management Corporation is a leading global alternative asset manager with approximately $572 billion in assets under management (AUM) and approximately 4,100 employees. The firm operates across credit, private equity, real assets, and secondaries strategies, serving institutional investors including large pension funds, university endowments, sovereign wealth funds, banks, and insurance companies.

The Capital Solutions Group at Ares focuses on liabilities sourcing and financing, asset sourcing, and strategic counterparty relationships across the entire Ares platform. This Associate position sits within the Real Estate Debt Capital Market team and will partner with Portfolio Management, Acquisitions, and Accounting teams on financing transactions, lending relationships, cash and liquidity reporting, forecasting, and hedging activities.

Key responsibilities include assisting with marketing, structuring, and negotiating financing arrangements, supporting debt capital markets activities for the real estate platform, coordinating with internal teams to manage portfolio liquidity, and contributing to interest rate hedging and risk management processes. The role offers direct exposure to senior leadership and a broad view of Ares real estate debt strategies across the capital stack.

Ares Management is an employee-focused firm committed to career development and fostering an inclusive, high-performance culture guided by core values of being Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, and Trustworthy.

Apply for this Position

New York, United States

June 1, 2026

Region

New York

Get instant notifications when new job openings matching "Private Credit / Senior Associate jobs in New York, United States" are published.

## About This Role

Ares Management is seeking a Senior Associate for its U.S. Direct Lending Special Situations team. The role supports control equity positions and workouts/restructurings within the U.S. Direct Lending portfolio, which has $193 billion in AUM.

The Senior Associate will have direct interaction with Ares senior leadership and portfolio company executives, offering significant long-term career growth opportunities. Core responsibilities include financial modeling and analysis supporting active restructurings and equity-owned portfolio companies, with experience in 3-statement operational modeling, liquidity analysis, and capital structure modeling. The role involves supporting performance improvement efforts for owned portfolio companies, conducting due diligence for active restructurings, reviewing and negotiating legal documentation, and communicating with management teams on strategy, budgeting, and financial performance.

The position also requires drafting and presenting Investment Committee memos to senior leadership and utilizing the firm proprietary software tools for company tracking.

Qualified candidates should have 4+ years of consulting, credit, private equity, or investment banking experience, with advanced Excel modeling and PowerPoint skills. Experience reading and analyzing credit, equity, and restructuring-related legal documents is essential. The role requires periodic travel and reports to a Partner in the Special Situations group.

Ares Management was founded in 1997 and has grown into one of the largest global alternative asset managers, driven by a disciplined investment philosophy focused on delivering compelling risk-adjusted returns throughout market cycles.

Apply for this Position

# Analyst/Associate, Portfolio Management, Direct Lending

Posted May 28, 2026

Ares Management is a leading firm focused on shared prosperity and a better future through its diverse team and inclusive culture. They are seeking an Analyst/Associate for their Direct Lending – Portfolio Management department, responsible for portfolio monitoring, valuation, analytics, and restructurings while collaborating with various departments across the firm.

### Responsibilities

- Monitor and manage 15-20 assigned corporate debt and equity investments
- Provide direct support to deal teams as necessary including assisting with re-underwrites, amendments, diligence on opportunities with active investments, and assisting with the preparation of investment committee memos
- General portfolio management including quarterly valuation and reporting
- Communicate with portfolio company management teams and sponsors to assist with the detailed monitoring of the portfolio (hold lender calls and diligence calls, attend board meetings, etc.)
- Utilize firm’s proprietary software tools for company tracking of KPIs
- Perform mark-to-market quarter end valuations
- Analyze portfolio trends and track relevant current market activities
- Develop and produce periodic and ad hoc reports and presentations for both internal (investment team, senior management, board of directors, investor relations) and external target audiences (investors, limited partners)

Skills

- Bachelor's degree or international equivalent
- 1-3 years of finance or accounting experience with exposure to valuation methods required
- Advanced MS Excel modeling skills (v-lookup, macros, and pivot tables)
- Beginning to intermediate MS PowerPoint skills
- Self-motivated and driven; ability to work effectively in a collegial, fast moving, demanding, and dynamic environment
- Creative problem solver
- Exceptional interpersonal skills
- Impeccable integrity and trustworthiness

### Benefits

- Comprehensive Medical/Rx
- Dental and Vision plans
- 401(k) program with company match
- Flexible Savings Accounts (FSA)
- Healthcare Savings Accounts (HSA) with company contribution
- Basic and Voluntary Life Insurance
- Long-Term Disability (LTD) and Short-Term Disability (STD) insurance
- Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
- Commuter Benefits plan for parking and transit
- Access to a world-class medical advisory team
- A mental health app that includes coaching, therapy and psychiatry
- A mindfulness and wellbeing app
- Financial wellness benefit that includes access to a financial advisor
- New parent leave
- Reproductive and adoption assistance
- Emergency backup care
- Matching gift program
- Education sponsorship program

Company Overview

- Ares Management is an alternative asset manager focusing on credit, private equity, and real estate. It was founded in 1997, and is headquartered in Los Angeles, California, USA, with a workforce of 1001-5000 employees. Its website is http://www.aresmgmt.com. Company H1B Sponsorship

## About This Role

Ares Management is seeking an Associate for its Real Estate Asset Management team, based in London. The role focuses on driving value creation, monitoring performance, and executing business plans across a diversified European portfolio with a primary focus on pan-European retail and office assets.

The Associate will work closely with senior asset managers, investment teams, finance, and third-party operators and servicers to ensure high-quality analysis, reporting, governance, and disciplined execution throughout the asset lifecycle. The position supports assets across Europe, working across the full range of strategies including direct holdings, joint ventures, and co-investment vehicles.

Key responsibilities include managing asset-level business plans in partnership with the wider deal team and external operating partners, monitoring operating performance (NOI, occupancy, leasing velocity, renewals, arrears, operating expenses), and coordinating annual budgeting and quarterly IRR reforecasting processes. The role also involves helping identify and execute value creation initiatives, monitoring capital expenditure programs, and supporting acquisitions onboarding, financing, refinancing, and exit execution.

The position offers exposure to a leading global real estate investment platform and requires strong financial analysis, data integrity, and communication skills. Ares Management has approximately $572 billion in AUM and is guided by core values of Collaborative, Responsible, Entrepreneurial, Self-Aware, and Trustworthy.

results.title: Data Scientist Associate- NLP & LLM – NLP People
highlights:
Data Scientist Associate- NLP & LLM – NLP People

## Overview

JPMAM Data Science is dedicated to enhancing and streamlining every stage of the investment process, from financial analysis and portfolio management to client services and advisory. We analyse extensive collections of data including financial documents, analyst reports, news articles, meeting notes, and client communications to empower data-driven decision making and process automation. We are seeking passionate data scientists who can apply the latest methodologies to generate real-time and actionable insights.

The candidate must excel in working in a highly collaborative environment together with the business stakeholders, technologists, and control partners to deploy solutions into production. A strong passion for machine learning is essential, along with a commitment to continuous learning, research, and experimentation with new innovations in the field. Candidates should possess a solid understanding of modern NLP and/or financial knowledge, hands-on implementation experience, strong analytical thinking, and a keen interest in applying advanced analytics to solve complex problems in finance and asset management.

Job Responsibilities

Design and implement advanced techniques such as semantic search, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), named entity recognition (NER), prompt engineering, and personalization for content extraction, search, question answering, reasoning, and recommendation. Develop LLM, NLP, and ML solutions that address client requirements and drive business transformation. Work closely with partner teams—including Business, Technology, Product Management, Strategy, and Business Management—to deploy and scale developed models in production environments. Build comprehensive testing setups to evaluate model performance and ensure efficacy and reliability. Communicate results effectively to business stakeholders through written reports, visualizations, and presentations. Stay current with the latest research in LLM, ML, and data science, and proactively identify and leverage emerging techniques to drive ongoing enhancement.

Required Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills

Advanced degree in Data Science, Computer Science, or Machine LearningProven experience applying NLP, LLM, and ML techniques to solve high-impact business problems. Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and familiarity with machine learning libraries and frameworks. Excellent communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, dynamic environment. Strong analytical skills with an understanding of financial markets and asset management.

Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities and Skills.

AI Engineer (12-Month FTC) Location: London (Hybrid) Duration: 12-Month Fixed-Term Contract (Strong Potential for Permanent Conversion) Salary: £100,000 + Bonus & Benefits Overview We are seeking a highly capable Applied AI & Automation Engineer to join a growing Data Science team within a leading hedge fund investment specialist. This role offers the opportunity to design and build an intelligent reporting and operational automation platform from the ground up, transforming how fund administration processes are executed. Working closely with the Head of Data Science and Fund Administration stakeholders, you will apply modern AI, automation, and data engineering techniques to replace manual processes with scalable, auditable, and production-grade systems. The successful candidate will play a key role in modernising legacy reporting and reconciliation workflows, implementing AI-assisted automation, and establishing the foundation for future AI-driven operational capabilities. This is a highly technical, hands-on engineering role suited to someone who enjoys solving complex operational challenges and building robust systems that deliver measurable business value. results.title: AI Engineer | Wfh
highlights:
AI Engineer | Wfh

Wfh.← All Jobs

# AI Engineer

Posted Jun 5, 2026

Lazard is one of the world’s preeminent financial advisory and asset management firms. The AI Engineer will work within the AI & Data Group to design, develop, and deploy advanced AI-driven applications and solutions to support the Firm’s financial advisory and asset management businesses.

### Responsibilities

- Build large-scale AI and machine learning projects that deliver actionable insights and drive innovation
- Collaborate closely with investment bankers, asset managers, data scientists, software engineers, and senior management across the Firm to deliver impactful solutions

Skills

- Bachelor's or advanced degree in Computer Science, AI, Data Science, Machine Learning, or related fields
- 1-3 years of experience developing large scale GenAI applications
- Expertise in Python and frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and scikit-learn
- Deep knowledge of machine learning algorithms and experience in productionizing AI models
- Proficiency with frameworks for large language models (LLMs) such as LangChain, LlamaIndex, and VectorDBs
- Experience developing and deploying AI solutions using cloud platforms like Azure, AWS, or GCP
- Advanced knowledge of unstructured data processing, including NLP (e.g., sentiment analysis, entity recognition, topic modeling, and retrieval-augmented generation)
- Proficiency in APIs and microservices frameworks like FastAPI or Flask
- Demonstrated ability to query and analyze data from relational and NoSQL databases (e.g., PostgreSQL, MongoDB, ElasticSearch)
- Strong communication skills with the ability to translate technical findings into actionable business strategies for bankers, asset managers, and other stakeholders
- Passion for building innovative, high-quality AI-driven products
- Ability to work both independently and collaboratively
- Prior experience in the financial industry or a strong interest in finance
- Familiar with Databricks, Azure AI and Microsoft Copilot ecosystem
- Expertise in building advanced retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems and developing autonomous LLM agent workflows
- Familiarity with MLOps workflows and tools like MLflow, Kubeflow, or Vertex AI
- Experience with real-time AI applications and stream processing frameworks
- Demonstrated success in delivering AI solutions with measurable business impact
- Strong knowledge of advanced GenAI and communicating insights to non-technical audiences

### Benefits

- Comprehensive, competitive benefits

Company Overview

- Lazard is a financial advisory and asset management firm focused on income strategies, alternative investments, and private equity funds. It was founded in 1848, and is headquartered in New York, New York, USA, with a workforce of 1001-5000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship

- Lazard has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 37 in 2025, 27 in 2024, 24 in 2023, 34 in 2022, 24 in 2021, 36 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

results.title: AI Strategy & Implementation Intern – Career Services | UNLV
highlights:
Brasa is seeking an AI Strategy & Implementation Intern to help lead the firm’s exploration, evaluation, and deployment of artificial intelligence solutions across the organization. This newly created position sits at the intersection of real estate investing, technology, operations, and innovation.
[...]
Working directly with the Investment Team and senior leadership, the Intern will help identify, evaluate, and implement practical AI solutions that improve efficiency, enhance decision-making, and create scalable competitive advantages across acquisitions, financial modeling, investor relations, reporting, marketing, and internal operations.
[...]
• Evaluate emerging AI tools, platforms, and technologies relevant to real estate investing and business operations.• Develop and test AI-powered workflows that improve efficiency across acquisitions, underwriting, asset management, investor relations, and corporate operations.• Build AI-assisted financial modeling, market research, and investment analysis processes.• Design custom GPTs, AI agents, prompt libraries, and automation tools for internal use.• Create templates and workflows that streamline document review, investment memos, reporting, and communication.• Assist with automating investor reporting, presentation preparation, and recurring administrative tasks.• Research best practices and emerging trends in artificial intelligence and provide recommendations to senior leadership.• Develop training materials and playbooks to support firm-wide AI adoption.• Measure and report on the effectiveness of implemented AI initiatives.• Partner with team members to pilot new technologies and identify additional opportunities for improvement.
[...]
Education• Current undergraduate, graduate, or MBA student at a leading university with a minimum GPA of 3.5.• Preferred fields of study include Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Economics, Finance, Business, Information Systems, or related disciplines.
[...]
Experience & Skills• Demonstrated interest in artificial intelligence, automation, and emerging technologies.• Hands-on experience using AI platforms such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, Copilot, or similar tools.• Strong analytical, research, and problem-solving abilities.• Excellent written and verbal communication skills.• Ability to work independently, manage multiple projects, and thrive in an entrepreneurial environment.• Interest in real estate, investing, financial services, or business operations strongly preferred.• Experience with SQL, Python, workflow automation tools, APIs, no-code platforms, or data analytics is a plus but not required.• Strong Microsoft Excel skills required; financial modeling experience is a plus.
[...]
Within the first 90 days, the Intern will be expected to identify several high-impact AI opportunities across the firm, develop and implement pilot workflows, and present recommendations for Brasa’s longer-term AI strategy and implementation roadmap.
[...]
In addition to a resume, candidates should submit a brief (1 page) overview of an AI project, workflow, automation, GPT, agent, or implementation they have personally built, designed, or deployed. The submission should describe:
[...]
Brasa is seeking candidates who are not only enthusiastic about artificial intelligence, but who have demonstrated initiative in applying AI tools to solve real-world business problems.

results.title: Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
highlights:
Senior / Principal Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
[...]
Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Senior or Principal Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will drive the development of our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.
[...]
design and evolution
[...]
Domain Translation: Translate biological and chemical domain knowledge into machine learning objectives, training signals, and evaluation criteria, working closely with domain experts.
[...]
Scalable Systems & Engineering: Architect and improve large-scale distributed machine learning systems, ensuring robustness, efficiency, and reproducibility across training and evaluation workflows.
[...]
As a Principal ML Scientist:
[...]
You help define the long-term technical roadmap for scientific reasoning models, identifying new opportunities and setting priorities across initiatives.
[...]
You architect new initiatives
[...]
data modalities, guiding the technical direction of cross-functional projects across gRED.
[...]
You serve as a key technical authority for leadership, influencing how Genentech leverages generative AI to solve high-stakes problems in the therapeutic pipeline.
[...]
For Senior (SE6): 0 – 2+ years of industry or post-doc experience with a focus on deep learning.
[...]
For Principal (SE7): 5+ years of industry or post-PhD experience with a demonstrated track record of technical leadership and project ownership.
[...]
LLM Expertise: Extensive experience developing and training large-scale machine learning models, including approaches to improve domain understanding, reasoning capabilities, and model alignment.
[...]
Engineering: Strong software engineering skills and experience designing and operating large-scale or high-performance machine learning systems.
[...]
Experience with molecular modalities (e.g., protein sequences, chemical graphs, and structured molecular data) is highly valued but not required.
[...]
A public portfolio of research or significant contributions to open-source ML libraries.
[...]
A passion for applying frontier AI to drug discovery.
[...]
The expected salary range for this position, based on the primary location of San Francisco, is $167,400 – 310,800 of hiring range for the Senior Scientist, and $194,000 – 360,400 for the Principal Scientist. For the primary of location of New York City, $160,100 – 297,300 for the Senior Scientist, and $185,600 – 344,600 for the Principal Scientist. Actual pay will be determined based on experience, qualifications, geographic location, and other job-related factors permitted by law. A discretionary annual bonus may be available based on individual and Company performance. This position also qualifies for the benefits detailed at the link provided below.

results.title: Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery) - Xtalks
highlights:
## Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery)
[...]
A healthier future. It's what drives us to innovate. To continuously advance science and ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need today and for generations to come. Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. We are seeking talented engineers with a passion for building large-scale, distributed machine learning algorithms and systems that will transform the drug discovery process. AIDD's Foundation Model team is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Engineer to develop the backend of our internal agents platform and enable the next generation of AI/ML-powered drug discovery applications as part of our broader Lab-in-the-Loop approach. We are looking for someone who is not only passionate about technical problem-solving but also has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions in AI/ML. The ideal candidate should have extensive experience in leading and managing a team of engineers and scientists, and be able to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence across the broader Roche organization. The group provides a dynamic and challenging environment for multidisciplinary research, including access to heterogeneous data sources, close links to top academic institutions around the world, as well as collaborations with internal Genentech and Roche teams. In this role, you will:

highlights:

# Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II
[...]
We’re hiring a Agentic and Generative Data Scientist II to design and deploy AI systems end-to-end: from data preparation and evaluation to model fine-tuning, inference, and agentic workflows. You’ll partner closely with product and engineering to ship reliable, cost-effective, and scalable LLM-powered solutions on AWS.
[...]
- End-to-end GenAI solutions: Scope problems, choose the right approach(prompt engineering, fine-tuning, agents), implement, evaluate, and deploy.
[...]
- Data & SQL: Write efficient SQL for analytics and data preparation; manage schemas and pipelines for model training and inference.
[...]
- Model training & fine-tuning: Run supervised fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA), optimize prompts, and manage experiment tracking and evaluation.
[...]
- Agentic systems: Build agent workflows with tool use, memory, and safety/guardrails.
[...]
- Inference & deployment: Package services with Docker, optimize latency/cost (batching, caching, quantization), and deploy on AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda with GPU acceleration).
[...]
- MLOps & Observability: Set up CI/CD for models/prompts, maintain offline/online evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and rollback strategies.
[...]
- Security & compliance: Implement data governance, PHI/PII protections, and guardrails against prompt injection and unsafe outputs.
[...]
- Cross-functional work: Collaborate with product managers and engineers to align GenAI capabilities with product goals; document clearly and communicate trade-offs.
[...]
- Production readiness: Lead conversations around scaling, monitoring, and maintaining GenAI systems in real-world environments.
[...]
### Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities)
[...]
- 5+ years of Software/ML engineering experience, including 2+ years building and deploying GenAI/LLM systems.
- MS/PHD in Computer Science or equivalent experience.
[...]
- Strong SQL and Python skills with solid software engineering fundamentals.
- Experience with agent frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI) and building tool-driven agents.
- Hands-on with deep learning (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and LLM fine-tuning(SFT/PEFT like LoRA/QLoRA).
- Production experience with Docker and deploying on AWS (ECS, EKS,SageMaker, Lambda, or GPU services).
[...]
- Experience creating Data and Model pipelines for model training anddeployment at scale.
[...]
- Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation frameworks (LLM-as-judge,metrics), and offline test harnesses.
[...]
- Understanding of security & compliance for sensitive data (e.g., PHI/PII) and safe deployment of AI systems.
[...]
- Excellent problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills.
[...]
- Inference optimization: quantization (bitsandbytes, GPTQ/AWQ), batching, caching, or vLLM.
[...]
- Experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B), CI/CD for ML, and monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana).
[...]
- Familiarity with major LLM APIs and OSS models (OpenAI, Anthropic, Llama, Mistral).
[...]
- Languages: Python, SQL
- Data: Snowflake, Postgres
- Cloud: AWS (ECS, EKS, SageMaker, Lambda)
- MLOps: Docker, CI/CD, MLflow or W&B
[...]
- Xsolis is an AI-driven technology company with a human-centered approach, fostering collaboration between healthcare providers and payers through real-time transparency, objective data for increased accuracy and alignment of medical necessity decisions, and more efficient outcomes. Dragonfly®, its AI-driven proprietary platform, is the first and only solution to use real-time predictive analytics to continuously assign an objective medical necessity score and assess the anticipated level of care for every patient, enabling more efficiency across the healthcare system. Xsolis is headquartered in Franklin, Tennessee.

# Senior Data Scientist/AI Engineer - Generative AI

Senior Data Scientist/AI Engineer - Generative AIEXO ARNVIND is collaborating with a pioneering financial technology leader in Sweden to identify their next Senior Data Scientist/AI Engineer specialising in Generative AI. Our client is shaping the future of AI-driven solutions in the financial sector, where cutting-edge technology meets real-world impact. This is a rare chance to join a high-caliber team, work on transformative generative AI projects, and influence how AI is deployed at scale in a regulated, high-stakes environment. If you are passionate about building production-grade AI systems and want to work where innovation meets execution, this role is for you.The OpportunityAs a Senior Data Scientist/AI Engineer focused on Generative AI, you will play a pivotal role in designing, developing, and deploying AI applications that redefine how AI is leveraged in financial services. You will collaborate with a talented, multidisciplinary team to create robust, scalable, and compliant AI solutions. This position offers the opportunity to work on high-impact projects, mentor colleagues, and establish best practices in generative AI-all while operating in a dynamic, forward-thinking organisation.Key Responsibilities
